---
title: "Question 4"
---

We utilise the `foreach` and `inorm` functions to iterate over 3 vectors, each containing 5 random variables, additionally, we find the largest value in each vector and print out these large values.

```{r}
#| warning: false
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)
library(iterators)

```

```{r}
#| warning: false
set.seed(1234)

# Set up parallel backend
cl <- makeCluster(2)  # even though the question doesn't specify, 2 is a safe value to use
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Iterate over 3 vectors, each with 5 random normals
# Find the largest value in each vector
result <- foreach(x = irnorm(5, count = 3), .combine = c) %dopar% {
  max(x)
}

# Clean up
stopCluster(cl)

# Print the largest values
print(result)
```
